steps.title: Identify the signal marking a possible misconception
steps.content: The phrase “may likewise seem to have been pioneering” immediately after the underlined portion signals a possible misconception. The underlined portion must introduce the information that creates this false impression.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content:
The underlined portion links Randall's broadsides to the Black Arts Movement's innovative aesthetics.

This connection could lead readers to mistakenly believe Randall's format was pioneering.

The passage then corrects this misconception with “but, in fact,” revealing that broadside poetry existed in seventeenth-century London.

The other answers misidentify what the passage undermines (A), focus on the movement rather than the misconception (C), or point to context already given (D).

This matches choice B — B is correct.
